hero.title: بداية حياة - BEDAYET HAYA
hero.subtitle: A comprehensive and reliable guide for mothers to care for newborns based on the latest global medical recommendations

quick_access.title: Quick Access to Information
quick_access.subtitle: Everything you need to care for your baby in one place

features.title: Important Topics
features.vaccination_desc: Mandatory vaccination schedule for the first six months and how to deal with side effects
features.diseases_desc: Recognize common diseases and danger signs like sepsis and how to respond
features.growth_desc: Track your baby's growth, calculate appropriate weight for age, and developmental milestones
features.care_desc: Personal hygiene, diaper care, bathing, and general baby cleanliness

monthly_guide.title: Your Monthly Guide
monthly_guide.subtitle: Your baby's development month by month

tips.title: Golden Tips for Mothers
tips.subtitle: Important tips for proper baby care
tips.tip1_desc: Every child is unique and different. Be patient and loving, as this is the foundation of healthy growth.
tips.tip2_desc: Put your baby to sleep on their back, in a crib free of toys and blankets.
tips.tip3_desc: Take care of your nutrition and health, as this affects the quality of your milk and your mental health.
tips.tip4_desc: Don't hesitate to ask for help from family and friends. Childcare is a shared responsibility.
tips.tip5_desc: Take photos and record important moments. Time passes quickly and these memories are precious.
tips.tip6_desc: Motherhood is a journey full of challenges and joy. Enjoy every moment with your baby.

header.subtitle: Your complete guide for the first 6 months

footer.site_desc: A comprehensive and trusted guide for mothers to care for newborns in the first six months.
footer.designed_by: BEDAYET HAYA | Designed with care and love for mothers and babies
footer.desc: Comprehensive and reliable guide for mothers to care for newborns in the first six months.

vaccination.title: Vaccination Schedule
vaccination.subtitle: Complete schedule of mandatory vaccinations for the first 18 months with important side-effect instructions

month6.desc: Oral & Injected Polio + Pentavalent + Vitamin A (100k IU)

month12.desc: Oral Polio + MMR Vaccine + Vitamin A (200k IU)

month18.desc: Oral Polio + MMR + DTP Booster + Vitamin A (200k IU)

side_effects.title: Side Effects & How to Handle Them

fever.title: High Temperature
fever.handle: Use lukewarm compresses and paracetamol based on weight (after medical advice)

pain.title: Swelling at Injection Site
pain.desc: Redness and slight swelling for 2-3 days
pain.handle_new: Apply cold compresses initially, then warm compresses for 15 minutes.

mood.title: Irritability & Crying
mood.desc: Increased crying and irritation for 24-48 hours
mood.handle_new: Continuous breastfeeding is crucial during and after vaccination to soothe the child.

warning.title: Danger Signs - Call Doctor Immediately
warning.convulsions_desc: Occurrence of convulsions or sudden loss of consciousness
warning.abscess_desc: Appearance of an abscess or pussy lump at the injection site

opv.title: Important Notes on Oral Polio Vaccine (OPV)
opv.breastfeeding: Breastfeeding does not affect the Oral Polio Vaccine.
opv.spit: If the child spits out the vaccine, it can be re-administered safely.
opv.health: The vaccine should be given when the child is completely healthy.
opv.diarrhea: Diarrhea is one of the conditions that may make vaccines ineffective.

tips_before.title: Tips Before Vaccination
tips_before.t1: Inform doctor if child is sick or has allergies
tips_before.t2: Breastfeed 30 mins before vaccination to soothe
tips_before.t4: Avoid vaccinating the child (especially oral vaccines) if they have diarrhea, as it prevents effective absorption, requiring the dose to be repeated after recovery.

diseases.title: Diseases & Danger Signs
diseases.subtitle: Comprehensive guide to common diseases, danger signs, and emergencies in infants

common.title: Common Diseases in the First Months

respiratory.title: Respiratory Diseases
respiratory.desc: Observe danger signs like rapid breathing and abnormal chest sounds (wheezing or whistling). Consult a doctor immediately.

digestive.title: Digestive System Diseases
digestive.desc: Diarrhea, colic, and reflux. Monitor frequency and stool consistency.

dehydration_treatment.title: Dehydration Management Protocol
dehydration_treatment.step1: Administer Oral Rehydration Salts (ORS) at home as per medical advice.
dehydration_treatment.step2: Continue breastfeeding or formula feeding regularly.
dehydration_treatment.step3: Give Zinc syrup to reduce the severity of diarrhea.
dehydration_treatment.step4: Maintain strict hygiene for hands and feeding tools.

skin.title: Skin & Oral Conditions
skin.desc: Mainly Oral Thrush (dense white patches on tongue and inner cheeks) and diaper rash.

danger_signs.title: Danger Signs
danger_signs.breathing: Breathing difficulty (flaring nostrils or chest pulling in)
danger_signs.feeding: Inability to breastfeed or persistent vomiting of everything
danger_signs.neuro: Seizures, severe lethargy (hard to wake), or high-pitched crying

dehydration.title: Signs of Dehydration
dehydration.mild: Dry mouth and fewer tears when crying
dehydration.moderate: Sunken eyes and fewer wet diapers (less than 6)
dehydration.severe: Sunken fontanelle (soft spot on head) and inelastic skin

emergency.title: When to Go to Emergency Immediately?
list: Bleeding from anywhere in the body
Blue tint on tongue or lips

growth.title: Growth & Development
growth.subtitle: Comprehensive guide to monitoring physical, mental, and motor development in the first 6 months

weight_height.title: Weight, Height & Head Circumference Rates
weight_height.desc: Every baby grows at their own pace, but there are general averages doctors follow

weight.title: Weight
weight.desc: Weight increases by 150-200 grams weekly in the first months

height.title: Height
height.desc: Height increases by approximately 2.5 cm monthly

head.title: Head Circumference
head.desc: Important index for brain development, measured at each visit

m1.title: Motor Development
m1.desc: Starts with head lifting, then rolling, sitting with support, and without it by month 6

m2.title: Visual & Auditory Development
m2.desc: Following moving objects, recognizing parents' voices, responding to sudden sounds

m3.title: Social Communication
m3.desc: Social smile (month 2), loud laughter, and making (cooing) sounds

calculator.title: Ideal Weight Calculation
calculator.note: This is an approximate formula; medical growth charts are more accurate

warning.title: Signs of Developmental Delay
warning.w1: Cannot hold head steady by 4 months
warning.w3: Does not respond to sounds or light

care.title: Daily Care
care.subtitle: Everything you need to know about personal hygiene, bathing, and complete baby care

face.title: Face & Head Cleaning
face.desc: Use a damp cotton ball with warm water to wipe eyes from inside out and clean head gently

skin.title: Skin Folds
skin.desc: Pay attention to drying the neck, underarms, and behind ears to prevent infections

diaper.title: Diaper Area
diaper.desc: Change diaper as soon as soiled, wash and dry area well, and use protective cream

bath.title: Safe Bathing Steps
bath.prep: Prepare all supplies before undressing baby and check water temp (37°C)
bath.step1: Support baby's head and neck well throughout the bath
bath.step2: Use tear-free baby soap and shampoo
bath.step3: Dry quickly and gently, covering the head to prevent heat loss

umbilical.title: Umbilical Cord Care
umbilical.desc: Keep it dry and clean, fold diaper below it, consult doctor if there is pus or odor

nails_hair.title: Nails & Hair
nails_hair.nails: Trim nails carefully with baby clippers to prevent scratching
nails_hair.hair: Comb with a soft brush, treat cradle cap with baby oil

diaper.title: Diaper Care
diaper.step1_desc: Prepare all necessary tools: clean diaper, wet wipes, barrier cream, old diaper
diaper.step2_desc: Open the old diaper and use the clean part to wipe stool from top to bottom
diaper.step3_desc: Clean the area well with wet wipes or warm water and mild soap
diaper.step4_desc: Dry the area completely with a soft towel, focusing on skin folds
diaper.step5_desc: Apply diaper rash cream if necessary
diaper.step6_desc: Place the clean diaper and ensure it is comfortable and secure

hair.cradle_cap_desc: Yellowish greasy patches on the scalp

evening.title: Evening (18:00 - 24:00)
evening.songs: Quiet songs or a short story

breastfeeding.title: Breastfeeding
breastfeeding.subtitle: Comprehensive guide to breastfeeding, correct techniques, and solving common problems

cradle.title: Cradle Hold
cradle.desc: Traditional and most common, baby's head in the crook of your arm

football.title: Football Hold
football.desc: Ideal after C-section and for twins, baby under your arm

side.title: Side-Lying Position
side.desc: Ideal for night feeds and for resting after birth

attachment.title: Correct Latch Technique
list: Ensure baby takes large part of areola

sore.title: Sore Nipples
sore.solutions: Correct position, apply breast milk to nipple, lanolin cream

low_supply.title: Blocked Milk Ducts
low_supply.solutions: Increase feedings, rest, drink plenty of fluids

engorgement.title: Breast Engorgement
engorgement.causes: Milk building up too much in the breast
engorgement.solutions: Frequent feeds, warm compresses before feed, gentle massage

fluids.title: Fluids
fluids.desc: Drink 2-3 liters of water daily, and natural juices

maternal.title: Maternal Difficulties & How to Handle Them

breastfeeding.positions_note: There is no ideal breastfeeding position, but there is a comfortable position for the mother during breastfeeding. Choose the position that suits you best.
breastfeeding.refusal_note: Breastfeeding refusal is a temporary matter that resolves when the underlying cause is addressed.

safe_sleep.title: Safe Sleep
safe_sleep.pacifier_rule: Do NOT give a pacifier to your baby during the breastfeeding period
safe_sleep.pacifier_reason: Using a pacifier during the breastfeeding period may cause Nipple Confusion and negatively affect breastfeeding success.
safe_sleep.no_pacifier_bf: No Pacifier with Breastfeeding - Avoid giving a pacifier to your infant during the breastfeeding period.
safe_sleep.rule1: Always place baby on their back to sleep
safe_sleep.rule2: Firm crib with no pillows or toys

emergency.title: Emergency Procedures
emergency.fever_step1_desc: Apply lukewarm (not cold) water compresses on the baby's forehead, underarms, and between thighs.
emergency.fever_step2_desc: Give an age-appropriate fever reducer as per doctor's instructions (after applying compresses first).
emergency.fever_warning: If the fever persists or is above 38.5°C in an infant, see a doctor immediately.
emergency.choking_step1: The mother performs first aid for the child until emergency services arrive.
emergency.choking_step2: Perform back blows - 5 firm blows between the shoulder blades.
emergency.choking_step3: The goal is to dislodge the object the child has swallowed.
emergency.choking_warning: WARNING: Do NOT try to squeeze or press on the child's chest. Use the back blow technique only.
emergency.choking_call: Call emergency services immediately at 123 while performing first aid.

safety.title: Safety & Security
safety.subtitle: Comprehensive guidelines to ensure your baby's safety at home and outside

rules.title: Safe Sleep Rules
rules.back: Always sleep on back for every nap and night
rules.crib: Firm, safe mattress with only a fitted sheet
rules.empty: No pillows, toys, or blankets in the crib

danger.title: Risks to Avoid
danger.co_sleeping: Sleeping with parents in same bed is very dangerous
danger.soft_surface: Avoid sofas and waterbeds for sleep

car.title: Car Safety

seat.title: Car Seat
seat.desc: Use rear-facing seat until at least age 2 in the back seat

reminders: Never leave baby alone in the car

warning_signs.title: Warning Signs Requiring Immediate Medical Attention

followup.title: Medical Follow-up Schedule
followup.subtitle: Complete schedule of medical visits and periodic checkups in the first 6 months

bring.title: What to Bring to the Visit

monitoring.title: Growth Monitoring
monitoring.warning: Weight loss, no response to sound, severe lethargy

additional_features.breastfeeding_desc: Proper breastfeeding techniques, common issues, and how to deal with them
additional_features.safety_desc: Safety procedures, safe sleep, and avoiding household risks
additional_features.followup_desc: Medical visit schedules and periodic check-ups for the first six months

emergency_section.title: Emergency Cases
emergency_section.subtitle: Learn the danger signs that require immediate medical consultation

resources_section.title: Trusted Resources
resources_section.subtitle: Our information is approved by global medical sources
resources_section.unicef_desc: UNICEF - Support for child and maternal health

statistics_section.title: Facts & Figures
statistics_section.subtitle: Important information about infant care
statistics_section.sids_reduction: Reduction in SIDS with safe sleep positions

missing_followup_399: • No weight gain or weight loss
missing_followup_402: • Head circumference growing too fast or too slow
missing_followup_403: • Falling below the third percentile on growth charts
missing_followup_406: Parent FAQs
missing_followup_410: When should I worry about my baby's growth?
missing_followup_411: If baby is not gaining weight appropriately, losing acquired skills, or if growth is much slower than peers.
missing_followup_413: How often should I visit the doctor?
missing_followup_414: Visits in the first six months are typically at birth, 1 month, 2 months, 4 months, and 6 months.
missing_followup_416: What if I missed a vaccination?
missing_followup_417: Call the clinic immediately to schedule. Vaccines can be somewhat delayed but it's best to stick to the schedule.
missing_followup_419: How do I know my baby is developing normally?
missing_followup_420: Watch for developmental milestones. Each baby grows at their own pace, but should achieve most within range.
missing_followup_422: What are signs of developmental delay?
missing_followup_423: Not lifting head, not smiling, not interacting, not tracking objects, or losing acquired skills.
missing_followup_427: When to Call the Doctor Between Visits
missing_followup_438: Difficulty breathing or very fast breathing
missing_followup_459: Abnormal sleep or hard to wake
missing_followup_483: A comprehensive and reliable guide for baby care in the first six months of life

meta.title: Formula Feeding - Bedaya Hayat | BEDAYA HAYAT

formula.title: Formula Feeding
formula.subtitle: A comprehensive guide to preparing formula milk safely, appropriate amounts for infants by age, and safety tips.
formula.prep_subtitle: Following the correct steps ensures your baby's safety and health
formula.step1_desc: Wash your hands thoroughly with soap and water. Ensure the bottle and nipple are sterilized before use.
formula.step2_desc: Boil pure water and let it cool slightly until lukewarm (temperature should not exceed 70°C to preserve milk benefits).
formula.step3_desc: Pour the required amount of water into the bottle first before adding the powder to ensure accurate measurement.
formula.step4_desc: Use the scoop provided with the package. Add one scoop for every 30 ml of water (or as per manufacturer instructions).
formula.step5_desc: Close the bottle and shake well until the powder is completely dissolved and no clumps appear.
formula.step6_desc: Test the milk temperature by placing drops on the inside of your wrist. It should be lukewarm, not hot.
formula.safety_title: Important Safety Tips for Formula Feeding
formula.safety_tip1_desc: Discard any remaining milk in the bottle one hour after starting the feed due to bacterial growth.
formula.safety_tip2_desc: Prepared formula can be kept in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours if the child has not fed from it.
formula.safety_tip3_desc: Avoid heating the formula in the microwave as it heats the milk unevenly and may burn the baby's mouth.
formula.safety_tip4_desc: Do not dilute the milk with extra water or increase its concentration; both can harm your baby's kidneys or cause malnutrition.
